rulings.Case Title: YAN GROSSMAN VS. HIGH GROUND CONSTRUCTION, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Compliance With Document Production And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Friday, Mar-28-2025, Line 7, 2-PLAINTIFF YAN GROSSMAN'S Motion To Compel Compliance With Document Production And Sanctions. Plaintiff's motion to compel production in accord with defendant High Ground Construction, Inc.'s statement of compliance to Request For Production set 1, Nos. 5 and 7, is granted. Defendant represents that it has produced documents in its files but not from its insurance broker. A party must produce documents that are within its control and not merely its possession; an agent such as a broker may have documents that are within its control. Defendant shall produce the outstanding and responsive insurance documents or provide a verified statement that it has produced all responsive documents within its possession, custody, or control within 10 court days of entry of this order. The court does not find that Defendant acted with substantial justification but does find that the circumstances of the case, and defendant's principal's incapacity, as well as the sanctions the court has ordered in a companion order today, would make imposition of further sanctions on this motion unjust. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: YAN GROSSMAN VS. HIGH GROUND CONSTRUCTION, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Friday, Mar-28-2025, Line 7, 1-PLAINTIFF YAN GROSSMAN'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories Request For Sanctions. Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to Form Interrogatories, Set One - Construction, Nos. 304.1 and 304.3 is granted. The motion was unopposed. The requests at issue are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP Section 2017.010.) Plaintiff has shown that defendant High Ground Construction, Inc.'s responses are incomplete, and defendant has failed to demonstrate that any objection has merit. The court orders defendant to supplement its responses to 304.1 and 304.3 with a complete and straightforward answer within 10 court days of entry of this order. Responses shall be verified. Sanctions are mandatory unless the court finds that the party resisting discovery acted with substantial justification or other circumstances make imposition of a sanction unjust. (CCP Section 2030.290(c).) The court does not find substantial justification and orders Defendant to pay $3,041.50 in sanctions to Plaintiff within 10 court days of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. rulings.Case Title: CREDIT CORP SOLUTIONS INC., VS. JASON JONES ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Friday, Mar-28-2025, Line 10, CROSS DEFENDANT CREDIT CORP SOLUTIONS INC.'S, ASSIGNEE OF SYNCHRONY BANK, DEMURRER to 1ST Amended CROSS COMPLAINT. Cross-defendant's demurrer to the First Amended Cross-Complaint ("FACC") is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to causes of action one through four. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to cause of action five. A cross-complaint shall contain "[a] statement of the facts constituting the cause of action, in ordinary and concise language." (CCP 425.10(a)(1).) "[A] demurrer accepts as true all well pleaded facts and those facts of which the court can take judicial notice but not deductions, contentions, or conclusions of law or fact." (Fox v. JAMDAT Mobile, Inc. (2010) 185 Cal.App.4th 1068, 1078.) Here, the FACC is devoid of ultimate facts to support the claims. For example, the second cause of action disjunctively lists proscribed conduct in the most general of terms. There are no specifics regarding exactly what occurred in this case. In addition, a party must allege an unfair competition claim with reasonable particularity. (Khoury v. Maly's of California, Inc. (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 612, 619 ["A plaintiff alleging unfair business practices under these statutes must state with reasonable particularity the facts supporting the statutory elements of the violation."].) Plaintiff has 20 days leave to amend to allege ultimate facts to support causes of action one through four. The court sustains the demurrer to the declaratory relief cause of action without leave to amend. The claim is not "necessary or proper" given the circumstances of this case. (See CCP 1061 [court discretion to refuse declaratory relief]; Canova v. Trustees of Imperial Irrigation Dist. Employee Pension Plan (2007) 150 Cal.App.4th 1487, 1497 ["Declaratory relief operates prospectively to declare future rights, rather than to redress past wrongs . . . Where, as here, a party has a fully matured cause of action for money, the party must seek the remedy of damages, and not pursue a declaratory relief claim."].) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: JULIA PADILLA VS. AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Deposition And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Friday, Mar-28-2025, Line 8, PLAINTIFF JULIA PADILLA'S Motion To Compel Deposition And For Sanctions. Plaintiff's motion to compel deposition of defendant American Honda Motor Co., Inc.'s PMQ, with production of documents, is denied. Plaintiff filed and served her motion 15 court days before hearing. CCP 1008 requires 16 court days, with an additional two court days where service is made electronically. The court further notes that Plaintiff's meet and confer efforts about the PMQ topics and her document requests were inadequate. Plaintiff attempted to informally resolve her dispute about the date but did not attempt to informally resolve disputes about the topics and document requests. Moreover, Plaintiff's moving papers fail to "set forth specific facts showing good cause justifying the production for inspection of any document . . . described in the deposition notice." (CCP 2025.450(b)(1).) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
